differences.title: 1. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Reduces initial JS bundle size, improving perceived performance and time to interactive.
differences.rightDesc: Requires JavaScript download and execution on the client for initial rendering.

differences.title: 2. INTERACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Designed for static content or initial data fetching, not direct user interactions.
differences.rightDesc: Ideal for dynamic UIs, state management, and real-time user input handling.

differences.title: 3. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Can directly access backend resources and databases without client-side API calls.
differences.rightDesc: Fetches data from APIs running in the browser environment.

differences.title: 4. DEPENDENCIES
differences.leftDesc: Do not ship their JavaScript to the client, allowing for heavier backend dependencies.
differences.rightDesc: Can utilize any client-side JavaScript libraries and frameworks.

differences.title: 5. SEO
differences.leftDesc: Content is fully rendered on the server, making it easily discoverable by search engines.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on JavaScript execution for SEO, which can be less reliable for some bots.

differences.title: 6. RENDERING LOCATION
differences.leftDesc: Executed exclusively on the server, ideal for content-heavy pages and initial load.
differences.rightDesc: Executed entirely within the user's browser, suitable for complex interactivity.

differences.title: 7. CODE EXECUTION
differences.leftDesc: Code runs on the server, offering access to server-side logic and benefits.
differences.rightDesc: Code runs in the user's browser, enabling rich user interfaces and experiences.
